global_context.scene_description: A cinematic top-down overhead shot of a kitchen counter / desk surface in chaotic disarray on a hot Phoenix July afternoon — a homeowner's panic scene. The surface is clearly cluttered with FOUR smartphones, each with its screen lit up showing a different HVAC company's quote. Each phone screen displays a quote text/email with a generic HVAC company name and a high price. The four screens, going clockwise from upper-left: 'Desert Air HVAC — QUOTE: $14,500' / 'Cool Choice — QUOTE: $14,800' / 'AZ Climate Pros — QUOTE: $15,200' / 'Phoenix Heating Co. — QUOTE: $15,500'. Each price is in bold red on the screen. One phone is being held to a man's ear (his ear is visible at the right edge of the frame, hand gripping the phone) — that phone shows 'On Call: Sun State Cooling' and a notification 'CALLER ON HOLD — 8:23'. Other elements scattered on the counter add to the panic: a sweaty water bottle leaving a wet ring, several missed-call notifications, an open laptop showing a search results page for 'EMERGENCY HVAC PHOENIX', a pencil, a crumpled napkin, a small thermometer reading 92° (indoor temp). Lighting: harsh hot Phoenix afternoon sun spilling in from a window, slight orange-amber tint, sweat highlights on the man's hand. Mood: visceral panic, the chaos of being on the wrong side of demand. Cinematic overhead, theatrical lighting, NOT documentary photo, NOT cartoon — high-end editorial photo composition.

lighting.source: harsh hot afternoon light from window
lighting.quality: harsh, contrast-heavy, slight orange-amber tint, hot

composition.framing: top-down overhead, ~75-degree-down camera angle, captures full counter area with all phones visible
composition.focal_point: the four phone screens with their climbing quote prices
